caption: Battle AI phase 3 (#586): the general. Team 1 now has a general above its subcommander -- General.decide_army reads a doctrine profile (data/doctrines/*.json) once per AI tick and produces an army plan, a split into subcommander groups, and a reserve pool, threading the doctrine's rout-exploitation flag down to every unit leader. This clip forces the 'cautious' doctrine (reserve_fraction 0.4, reserve_commit_morale_threshold 30) and shows its reserve pool in action: two full-strength units (a Cavalry and an Infantry, bottom of frame) are held back from the start, HOLD_LINE-pinned at their own position while three weaker Infantry alone meet the enemy. As the front line takes losses, its weighted-average morale falls under the doctrine's threshold and General.decide_army folds the two reserves into the active line -- confirmed by state dump: still held at tick 240, current_order flipped from null to ATTACK by tick 300 (the decision reads state from before that tick's own combat, so the visible order change lands one AI-decision tick after the morale actually crosses the line). The reserves break their stationary hold, march up, and are in melee by tick 600-700. No new order types, no direct unit-state writes -- the general's decision reaches every unit exactly the same way a subcommander's or a unit leader's own decision does, as a real queue-visible order through Battle._apply_order_cmd.
